‘When I turned around, nothing was left’: One father's search for family after Venezuela quakes
Nelson Torrebella said a quick goodbye to his wife and two sons before jumping in his car. Then the earth shook violently beneath him.
Fire at Antwerp apartment block kills at least six
A man is seen clambering through a window to escape black smoke in the block where some 200 people live.
Village People frontman Victor Willis dies aged 74
Victor Willis was the band's frontman and co-wrote most of their hit songs including YMCA.
Madueke replaces Saka in England XI to face DR Congo in World Cup
Watch live as fans filter into Atlanta Stadium before England play DR Congo in the last 32 of the World Cup.
Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.
Canada celebrates its birthday as Mark Carney battles to keep it intact
As Canadians celebrate their nation's birthday, independence movements in two provinces are eyeing the exit from the country.
Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations
More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador.
'We're still standing': Americans on the state of the nation in its 250th year
The BBC spoke to US citizens across the country ahead of its big anniversary celebration this weekend.

Has football fever taken hold in the US?
With the World Cup now three weeks old, BBC Sport's reporters in the US look at how their perceptions have changed.
World Cup fans sweat through US 'heat dome'
As scorching heat left the US east coast baking in the middle of the World Cup, the BBC caught up with fans braving the weather to support their team.
United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass?
France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup.
Can the World Cup's viral stars turn social media fame into fortunes?
They’re not Ronaldo or Messi, but they might be set for life after gaining millions of new followers.

What we know about Taylor Swift's rumoured NYC wedding celebrations
The couple has been mum about their upcoming nuptials, but speculation has mounted about a multi-day celebration at Madison Square Garden.
US Supreme Court has dealt heavy defeats to Trump, while expanding his power
The birthright ruling brings to an end a Supreme Court term that has delivered the president some key victories.
A plane crashed into a tower in Beijing but China is not saying what happened
The collision left holes on the side of the tower, but Beijing has scrubbed other visible traces of the incident.
Watch: Mount Etna eruption creates trail of bright orange lava
A stream of thick orange lava was seen trailing from Europe's biggest active volcano, Mount Etna in Italy, snaking through the mountain.
What to know about the looming deadline on North American free trade
The US, Canada and Mexico were to reach an agreement on whether to extend their free trade pact by 1 July. All signs point to them blowing past that deadline.

US Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship in blow to Trump
The ruling is a major setback for Donald Trump's immigration agenda, and has been welcomed by civil rights groups.
Dangerous heatwave to hit US ahead of holiday weekend
North America will bake in extreme temperatures amid celebrations for the United States' 250th birthday.
Afghan Taliban launch strikes on border with Pakistan as tensions escalate
Pakistan's military says it shot down four rudimentary drones and will respond to any further provocation.
Zelensky in Dublin as Ireland begins EU presidency term
The presidency will last until December 2026 and means that Ireland will have a number of responsibilities.
Anthropic says US lifts export ban on its advanced AI tools
Fable and Mythos were abruptly suspended in June over concerns that they could be used by hackers.
Three-year-old rescued and taken to hospital six days after Venezuela quake
Video footage shows rescuers cheering as the child is pulled from rubble in La Guaira state.
Woman raped by Superdry co-founder tells BBC she was working for him at the time
She described feeling fear and dread as she returned to her job, working for James Holder, after the attack.

Wimbledon day three - follow live
Watch coverage, listen to radio commentary and follow text updates and latest scores from day three of Wimbledon 2026.
Serena Williams' hopes of playing alongside her older sister Venus in the Wimbledon doubles look in doubt because of a knee injury.
Chelsea sign Italy defender Palestra for £47m
Right-back Marco Palestra, 21, becomes Xabi Alonso's first signing as Chelsea manager.
Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.
LeBron's Last Dance - what next for James after Lakers exit?
LeBron James is entering free agency in the NBA this summer for what could be the final move of his storied 23-year career.
No signings, no sales - what is happening at Manchester United?
Manchester United have missed out on a second midfield target in Mateus Fernandes but are refusing to panic even though they are yet to sign any players this summer.
Concern over rise in online racist abuse at World Cup
The Word Cup has suffered a 'significant increase' in the most serious examples of racist abuse online, Fifa's social media protection service (SMPS) has found.

Watch coverage, listen to radio commentary and follow text updates and latest scores from day three of Wimbledon 2026.
Serena Williams' hopes of playing alongside her older sister Venus in the Wimbledon doubles look in doubt because of a knee injury.
Chelsea sign Italy defender Palestra for £47m

Right-back Marco Palestra, 21, becomes Xabi Alonso's first signing as Chelsea manager.
Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.
LeBron's Last Dance - what next for James after Lakers exit?
LeBron James is entering free agency in the NBA this summer for what could be the final move of his storied 23-year career.
No signings, no sales - what is happening at Manchester United?
Manchester United have missed out on a second midfield target in Mateus Fernandes but are refusing to panic even though they are yet to sign any players this summer.
Concern over rise in online racist abuse at World Cup
The Word Cup has suffered a 'significant increase' in the most serious examples of racist abuse online, Fifa's social media protection service (SMPS) has found.

3 hrs ago Europe Village People frontman Victor Willis dies aged 74 Victor Willis was the band's frontman and co-wrote most of their hit songs including YMCA. 6 hrs ago Culture LIVE Madueke replaces Saka in England XI to face DR Congo in World Cup Watch live as fans filter into Atlanta Stadium before England play DR Congo in the last 32 of the World Cup. Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office The president's crypto income far outpaces his earnings from real estate and Trump-themed items such as watches.

2 hrs ago Business Canada celebrates its birthday as Mark Carney battles to keep it intact As Canadians celebrate their nation's birthday, independence movements in two provinces are eyeing the exit from the country. 11 hrs ago US & Canada Three die in Mexico City World Cup celebrations More than one million people took to the streets of Mexico City on Tuesday to mark the country's win over Ecuador. 5 hrs ago Latin America 'We're still standing': Americans on the state of the nation in its 250th year The BBC spoke to US citizens across the country ahead of its big anniversary celebration this weekend.
11 hrs ago US & Canada Canada to make Eurovision Song Contest debut in 2027 Watch: Man comes face-to-face with bear inside truck Chris Brown ordered to pay $13m to housekeeper injured in dog attack Williams' knee injury leaves Wimbledon doubles in doubt Two UK police officers face misconduct investigation in case of murdered student Henry Nowak WORLD CUP Has football fever taken hold in the US? With the World Cup now three weeks old, BBC Sport's reporters in the US look at how their perceptions have changed.

World Cup fans sweat through US 'heat dome' As scorching heat left the US east coast baking in the middle of the World Cup, the BBC caught up with fans braving the weather to support their team. United, free and brilliant - can anyone stop France masterclass? France boss Didier Deschamps urges caution despite watching his side dismantle Sweden to leave Les Bleus fans dreaming of winning the World Cup. The ruling is a major setback for Donald Trump's immigration agenda, and has been welcomed by civil rights groups.

Wimbledon day three - follow live
Watch coverage, listen to radio commentary and follow text updates and latest scores from day three of Wimbledon 2026.
Serena Williams' hopes of playing alongside her older sister Venus in the Wimbledon doubles look in doubt because of a knee injury.
Chelsea sign Italy defender Palestra for £47m
Right-back Marco Palestra, 21, becomes Xabi Alonso's first signing as Chelsea manager.
Mexico rampant at fortress Azteca - and England could be next
Mexico have won all four games and not conceded a goal at this World Cup. Their last-16 opponents must also contend with playing at the Azteca Stadium.
LeBron's Last Dance - what next for James after Lakers exit?
LeBron James is entering free agency in the NBA this summer for what could be the final move of his storied 23-year career.
No signings, no sales - what is happening at Manchester United?
Manchester United have missed out on a second midfield target in Mateus Fernandes but are refusing to panic even though they are yet to sign any players this summer.
Concern over rise in online racist abuse at World Cup
The Word Cup has suffered a 'significant increase' in the most serious examples of racist abuse online, Fifa's social media protection service (SMPS) has found.
